contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Credential source-link click-through verified live — capoeira impact audit + jsDelivr stale-cache fix
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/27
  
  Details:
  Closed the oracle-to-credential loop end to end with live verification and two follow-on findings. (1) Capoeira impact audit: confirmed the permalink rewrite and backfill could not affect capoeira activity events — each backfill commit touched exactly one programs/truesight-grounding file (zero capoeira-tribo-mirim paths), the triggered build-cv-cache run concluded success with only the routine generated-at timestamp churn identical to every 6h cron run, oracle PR #27 changed only the oracle repo (capoeira has its own practice-event-submit.js), and the shared GAS practice_event_processing.gs was read but never modified. (2) Live click-through debugging: the credential page at truesight.me/programs/truesight-grounding/credentials/#pk-G0Q-GSnufn6_ still rendered bare URLs in .event-source-link because program-shell.js fetches CV JSONs primarily from jsDelivr (cdn.jsdelivr.net @main, 12h+ branch cache) and the CDN was serving the pre-backfill copy — the raw-GitHub fallback never engaged because jsDelivr was stale, not erroring. Fixed via jsDelivr purge API for all three practitioner CV files and verified each now serves the reading permalinks (2/1/6 across the three slugs); both of Gary's sessions (hex 6→40 Jun 3, hex 50→64 Jun 5) now click through to the oracle with hexagram revealed and QMDJ charted for the original cast moment. Flagged systemic follow-up candidate: every 6h CV rebuild faces the same jsDelivr lag; a purge step in build-cv-cache or fresh-fetch for CVs would eliminate it. Related PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/oracle/pull/27
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
